forecast-period.text: A high pressure system lingers about far western NSW, as a weak trough skims the southeast in the morning. A new high will move across waters to the south to be centred over Tasmania on Thursday, as it continues to extend a ridge over SA on Friday.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Cold to very cold for central and eastern districts with areas of morning frost and fog. Isolated showers about southern coastal districts and possibly developing over the Nullarbor Plain. Maximum temperatures below average. Northeast to southeasterly winds, tending west to southwesterly in the far southeast until the evening.

Isolated showers about parts of the southern coastal districts and over the Nullarbor Plain, possibly extending further into southern pastoral areas. Areas of patchy morning fog about the Eyre Peninsula and the eastern districts. Maximum temperatures below average. East to southeasterly winds, tending northeasterly in the far west.

Isolated light showers possibly about the Flinders and southern pastoral districts. Areas of morning fog about the Agricultural Areas. Maximum temperatures below average. East to southeasterly winds, tending northeasterly in the far west.

text.p: Isolated light showers on and east of the ranges. Possible morning fog about the ranges. Maximum temperatures below average. Northeast to southeast winds, tending east to northeasterly throughout the day.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Saturday: dry across the north, less than 2 mm for most districts, 2-5 mm about Kangaroo Island, the southeast districts and possibly Nullarbor and southern pastorals. Isolated falls of 5-15 mm possible about windward coasts of Kangaroo Island and the Lower South East district.
